name: Perplexity MCP Server
what_it_does: Perplexity AI MCP server enabling AI agents to access Perplexity's AI-powered search — querying Perplexity Sonar API for real-time web search with citations, getting AI-synthesized answers with source references, and integrating Perplexity's search-augmented LLM into agent-driven research and fact-checking workflows.
use_cases: Getting AI-synthesized answers with citations from research agents
Searching the web with real-time results from knowledge agents
Fact-checking and verifying information from accuracy agents
Finding current events and news from monitoring agents
Researching technical topics with cited sources from coding agents
Answering questions requiring real-time web data from assistant agents
not_for: Tasks not requiring web search (model has sufficient training data)
High-volume automated queries (Perplexity API has rate limits)
Teams preferring raw search results over AI-synthesized answers
best_when: An agent needs AI-synthesized answers with real-time web citations — better than raw search results for questions requiring synthesis across multiple sources.
avoid_when: You need raw search results without AI synthesis, or prefer Brave/Google Search APIs for unmodified web results.

auth.notes: Perplexity API key required. Available from perplexity.ai/settings/api. Sonar API uses bearer token authentication. Sign up at perplexity.ai.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Perplexity API: $5 initial credits on signup
pricing.paid_tiers: Sonar: $5/1M tokens input; Sonar Pro: $15/1M tokens; online models add per-search fees
pricing.notes: Perplexity API is usage-based. Initial free credits available. Sonar online model includes real-time web search per request fees. Community MCP server is free.

performance.rate_limits: Perplexity API: varies by tier; rate limits documented in Perplexity developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: Credit card required for Perplexity API despite initial free credits
Per-search costs accrue with online models — monitor usage to avoid surprise bills
Perplexity API versioning: Sonar model names change periodically
Response format includes citations array — agents must parse structured output
Community MCP (wysh3) — not official Perplexity server; may lag API changes
Latency is higher than raw search due to LLM processing of results
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key via bearer token. Community MCP. Store API key as environment variable. Perplexity API key has no scope restrictions — protect carefully.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Bearer token auth. Community MCP. Protect API key. No scope granularity on Perplexity API keys.
